$comment: Per-stage model routing for dynamic-pipeline. provider ∈ anthropic|openrouter|codex. Reconciled to AgenticJames's verified design (reels transcribed 2026-07-05, see larry/PIPELINE.md + larry/memory/reference/agenticjames-reel*.txt). Adds research (Gemini grounded search) + synthesize (Opus) stages; implement is split into implement_light (DeepSeek V4 via Opencoder) + implement_heavy (Codex GPT 5.5). Fable is opt-in at plan only; when not explicitly confirmed, plan falls back to opus. All slugs verified against OpenRouter /models 2026-07-05.

research.$comment: reel1 step1 — Gemini grounded search via Opencoder. External research stage.

explore.$comment: reel2 — parallel explore agents per domain; pair with Graphify on large repos.

synthesize.$comment: reel1 step2 — Opus 4.8 folds research/explore docs together before planning.

plan.$comment: reel1 step3 / reel2 — Fable 5 HIGH, stripped/lean context. Opt-in; falls back to opus.

implement_light.$comment: reel1 step4 (light) — cheaper coding impl on DeepSeek V4 via Opencoder.

implement_heavy.$comment: reel1 step4 (heavy) — heavy impl in Codex CLI via Codexer. NOTE (2026-07-07 dogfood): `codex exec --model gpt-5.5|gpt-5|gpt-5-codex` all return 400 under this ChatGPT-account auth ('model not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account'); only the account default works. Use the 'default' sentinel — runtime-bridge omits --model so the heavy arm runs on the codex account default.

implement.$comment: Legacy alias — weight-unaware callers land on the heavy arm (codex account default).

review.$comment: INVERTED review stack (Fable pick 2026-07-05, Josh-approved). Deterministic gates (build/test) run BEFORE any review token; reviewers are ALWAYS diff-scoped. Cheap cross-model lens first (always-on); Opus demoted to a capped, diff-only escalation pass.

primary.$comment: First lens — cheap non-Anthropic model, always-on, diff-scoped.

escalation.$comment: Second pass — Opus HIGH, diff-only, triggered ONLY when the primary lens flags or a deterministic gate fails. NEVER off-Anthropic.
